summary: Small symmetrical two-tower wooden building with a central raised curved roof section and three flat pagoda-like upper roofs. Exterior walls are primarily vertical wood planks with stone brick/stone slab accents at base and mid-level banding, nether brick (dark red) used for curved central roof, and glow blocks at roof centers. A low stone wall/fence runs around the perimeter on a dirt ground.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main volume with two taller vertical towers at opposite short ends and three raised roof plates (one central curved plate spanning between towers and two smaller plates above each tower).
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry along the long axis (mirrored towers and side features)

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: base coping, slab banding and decorative blocks around perimeter

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof edging and mid-level horizontal bands (stone slabs)

materials.name: brick
materials.role: possible alternative for stonebrick accents (lower confidence)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: perimeter low wall/fence elements between stone posts

materials.name: nether_brick
materials.role: central curved roof and smaller roof plates (dark red roof material)

elements: two tall corner towers with small square windows
central curved nether-brick roof spanning between towers
three flat layered roof plates with stone slab edging and glow blocks at centers
stone slab/stonebrick horizontal bands wrapping building
stone block peripheral posts and short connecting low wall
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular base footprint and raise two tall square towers at the short ends aligned centrally on each end.
Use vertical wood planks for walls with darker log blocks (or rotated wood) as corner/tower vertical lines; add small glass panes/windows in towers at regular vertical intervals.
Place stonebrick/stone slabs as a base course one block out from walls and as two horizontal band layers around mid-height; top the bands with slab_stone to form coping.

Build central curved roof from nether brick blocks creating a stepped arch between towers and cap each tower with a square layered flat roof: layered nether brick plates edged with stone slabs and a glow block in the center.
Surround building with 1-block-high stone posts and short connecting fence/stone slab pieces to mimic the low external wall seen in plan views.
uncertainties: Exact block types for stone accents: images show gray blocks that could be stonebrick, stone slabs, or regular stone—stonebrick + slab_stone is the best guess.
Precise interior layout and floor count: structure appears single-floor with tall towers but internal floors are not visible.
Dimensions are approximations from screenshots (width/depth/height may vary by ±2-4 blocks).
Some decorative details (e.g., fence vs. slab connectors around perimeter) are partially obscured and may be different in the original.
